title: Juvenilia, An Ode to Natural Beauty by Alan Seeger — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
There is a power whose inspiration fills
Nature's fair fabric, sun- and star-inwrought,
Like airy dew ere any drop distils,
Like perfume in the laden flower, like aught
Unseen which interfused throughout the whole
Becomes its quickening pulse and principle and soul.
Now when, the drift of old desire renewing,
Warm tides flow northward over valley and field,
meaning: "Juvenilia, An Ode to Natural Beauty" by Alan Seeger is a sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational poem consisting of 183 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature and inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "There is a power whose inspiration fills Nature's fair fabric, sun- and star-inwrought,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Alan Seeger's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
